# unch v0.2.1

This release hardens the Tree-sitter-based search flow with a reusable GitHub Actions workflow and path-based ranking weights for lower-signal files.

## Highlights

- moves the `searcher` implementation into a reusable workflow and turns generated `searcher.yml` files into thin wrappers
- pins generated CI wrappers to `uchebnick/unch` at `v0.2.1` instead of following `main`
- keeps the checked-in `unch` workflow self-hosted through the local reusable workflow while still publishing the remote index as before
- adds `.semsearch/file_weights.json` as a local ranking config used by search to downweight tests, examples, fixtures, mocks, generated outputs, and dependency directories
- expands the default file weighting patterns to cover common Go, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python test and example conventions
- improves lexical ranking in practice by letting canonical implementation files outrank example and test files when both match the same symbol

## Upgrade notes

- repositories that want the new thin CI wrapper can rerun `unch create ci` and commit the regenerated `.github/workflows/searcher.yml`
- repositories already using the full inlined workflow continue to work until they are regenerated; there is no schema break in this release
- `unch init`, `unch index`, and `unch search` now ensure `.semsearch/file_weights.json` exists locally
- `file_weights.json` is local repository state inside `.semsearch`, so adjust it per checkout if you want different ranking behavior
